name: Sufi dhikr ob3ect: the ritual remembrance chant where the name of God is repeated on the breath in a cyclic rhythm. FSPLIT: the dhikr splits the practitioner's consciousness into the outer self (the chanter, the body breathing) and the inner self (the heart where the Name resonates silently). FFUSE: when the dhikr deepens into fana (annihilation), the two selves reconstitute as one — the chanter disappears and only the Chanted remains. VINIT: silence before the first utterance. TANCH: the cycle of one complete breath (in and out). AFWD: the outward breath carrying the Name. AREV: the inward breath drawing the Name back into the heart. ENGAGR: the station of baqa after fana — the mystic is simultaneously annihilated in God and present in the world.

phase_0.domain_name: Sufi dhikr ob3ect: the ritual remembrance chant where the name of God is repeated on the breath in a cyclic rhythm. FSPLIT: the dhikr splits the practitioner's consciousness into the outer self (the chanter, the body breathing) and the inner self (the heart where the Name resonates silently). FFUSE: when the dhikr deepens into fana (annihilation), the two selves reconstitute as one — the chanter disappears and only the Chanted remains. VINIT: silence before the first utterance. TANCH: the cycle of one complete breath (in and out). AFWD: the outward breath carrying the Name. AREV: the inward breath drawing the Name back into the heart. ENGAGR: the station of baqa after fana — the mystic is simultaneously annihilated in God and present in the world.
phase_0.boundary_condition: the cycle of one complete breath (in and out)

VINIT.justification: void/uninitialized state before any naming or breath begins

TANCH.chosen_element: the cycle of one complete breath (in and out)
TANCH.justification: terminal anchor that contains and bounds the entire dhikr cycle

AFWD.chosen_element: the outward breath carrying the Name
AFWD.justification: forward morphism from the chanter outward into the world

AREV.chosen_element: the inward breath drawing the Name back into the heart
AREV.justification: reverse/descent morphism returning the Name inward

CLINK.chosen_element: the sequential linking of outward and inward breath in a continuous cycle
CLINK.justification: composition of AFWD then AREV forms a complete cycle

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the Name itself (Allah) as the constant identity
IMSCRIB.justification: self-recognition: the Name is always itself, unchanging through the cycle

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the dhikr splitting the practitioner's consciousness
FSPLIT.justification: splits into outer self (chanter, body breathing) and inner self (heart where Name resonates silently)

FFUSE.chosen_element: fana (annihilation) where the two selves reconstitute as one
FFUSE.justification: the chanter disappears and only the Chanted remains, recovering the original unified consciousness

EVALT.chosen_element: successful dhikr where the Name is remembered with presence
EVALT.justification: affirmative state: the practitioner is fully engaged

EVALF.chosen_element: distracted dhikr where the mind wanders
EVALF.justification: negative state: the Name is uttered but the heart is absent

ENGAGR.justification: paradox: simultaneously annihilated in God and present in the world

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of the dhikr in the soul's ledger
IFIX.justification: irreversible fixation: each breath's remembrance is eternally inscribed

phase_2.split_element: the dhikr splitting the practitioner's consciousness
phase_2.split_input: the practitioner's unified consciousness before the split
split_outputs: inner self (heart where Name resonates silently)
phase_2.fuse_element: fana (annihilation) where the two selves reconstitute as one
phase_2.fuse_result: the practitioner's unified consciousness after fana (only the Chanted remains)

phase_3.void_description: silence before the first utterance — uninitialized, no breath, no Name
phase_3.true_description: the Name is remembered with full presence — affirmative state
phase_3.false_description: the Name is uttered but the heart is absent — negative state
phase_3.both_description: baqa after fana — simultaneously annihilated and present

steps.domain_action: Step 1 IMSCRIB: the practitioner recognizes the Name as the constant identity
Step 2 AREV: the practitioner draws the Name inward into the heart
Step 3 FSPLIT: the dhikr splits consciousness into outer self and inner self
Step 4 AFWD: the outward breath carries the Name into the world
Step 5 FFUSE: in fana, the two selves fuse back into one unified consciousness
Step 6 CLINK: the outward and inward breaths are linked into a continuous cycle
Step 7 IFIX: the dhikr is permanently recorded in the soul's ledger
Step 8 IMSCRIB: the practitioner closes by recognizing the Name as the constant identity

phase_5.compiler_frontend: the spiritual guide (sheikh) who translates intention into practice
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: the breath as the medium of communication between outer and inner self
phase_5.memory_mechanism: the heart (qalb) where the Name is stored and retrieved
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: the rhythmic cycle of inhalation and exhalation
phase_5.alfs_store: the Quran and the teachings of the Prophet as the canonical bootstrap store

phase_6.pre_cycle_state: the practitioner's consciousness in a dispersed state
phase_6.post_cycle_state: the practitioner's consciousness unified in remembrance
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — the cycle conserves the Name as the invariant identity through transformation
